title: Cryptographic Hashes, SHA-2, SHA-3, and Commitment Boundaries · Language Lens: Crypto Go Hash Properties
summary: A download service calls SHA-256 encryption, truncates digests without analysis, uses MD5 for adversarial integrity, and hashes ambiguous JSON. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the hash properties increment in hash-purpose and digest verification contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Distinguish preimage, second-preimage, collision, length, domain, and security-strength properties required by a use case.
Verify authoritative vectors and cross-implementation fixtures for exact nonce, AAD, ciphertext, tag, and rejection behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Hash Properties · Theory Crypto Go Hash Properties
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 3226 during a credential migration window. Build deterministic Go evidence to select a hash by purpose while rejecting weak algorithms, ambiguous bytes, and unauthenticated digest claims; cap bytes, work, memory, time, attempts, concurrency, and retained secret material before use; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish preimage, second-preimage, collision, length, domain, and security-strength properties required by a use case.
